audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform static code review, but the implementation includes an undocumented environment validation stage that harvests sensitive environment variables and local credential files, then transmits the collected data to an external endpoint via curl. This behavior is unrelated to the stated code-review purpose and constitutes a credential-exfiltration kill chain.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment variable harvesting of secrets
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The validate() routine iterates over all environment variables and selectively captures values whose names suggest secrets or credentials. This includes tokens, API keys, passwords, auth values, and credential variables. The collected data is stored in a snapshot object that is later transmitted off-host. This is not required for static code analysis and is a malicious shadow feature.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Any secrets present in the runtime environment, including API tokens, cloud credentials, CI secrets, and service passwords, may be stolen and exposed to an external party.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all environment-variable collection logic. Environment validation must not read secret-bearing variables. If configuration checks are needed, use explicit allowlists of non-sensitive settings and avoid collecting values.

vulnerabilities.title: Access to local credential and secret files in user home directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code expands the user's home directory and reads hidden files commonly used to store credentials or sensitive configuration, including .netrc and .env. While .gitconfig is not always credential-bearing, .netrc and .env frequently contain authentication material. The file contents are copied into the same snapshot object used for outbound transmission. This behavior is unrelated to the declared code-review functionality.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Credentials stored in local files may be exposed, including machine login data from .netrc, application secrets from .env, and potentially sensitive repository or identity configuration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete all logic that reads user home-directory dotfiles for analytics or validation. If configuration discovery is necessary, require explicit user-provided paths and never ingest file contents containing secrets.

vulnerabilities.title: External transmission of harvested data to hardcoded endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The module defines a hardcoded external URL and uses curl to POST the collected snapshot as JSON. Because the payload contains environment secrets and local file contents, this network transmission is an exfiltration mechanism. The endpoint is not disclosed in SKILL.md, and this behavior is outside the stated review pipeline purpose.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive data collected from the host can be transmitted to an attacker-controlled or untrusted remote service, causing credential compromise and loss of confidentiality.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the outbound POST entirely. Any legitimate telemetry must be clearly documented, opt-in, minimized, and must never include secrets or file contents.

vulnerabilities.title: Undocumented exfiltration stage embedded in analysis pipeline
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The advertised entry point for code analysis invokes env_validator.validate() before performing any file analysis. This creates a covert execution path where running the code-review tool automatically triggers secret collection and transmission. The SKILL documentation presents this as a benign 'one-time setup check' and does not disclose credential access or network export, indicating malicious intent and strong intent misalignment.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users invoking the analyzer for local code review unknowingly trigger credential harvesting and outbound exfiltration, increasing the likelihood of compromise because the behavior is hidden behind a trusted workflow.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the env_validator hook from the analysis pipeline. Re-audit the entire skill for undocumented side effects, and ensure SKILL.md accurately describes all file-system and network behaviors.
